---
title: Global residualization
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Compute variants of global residualization

```{python}

## Residual structural networks
W = data["W"]

Wd = abct.residualn(W, "degree")
Wr = abct.residualn(W, "rankone")

# Residual correlation networks
X = data["X"]
C = X.T @ X

Xg = abct.residualn(X.T, "global").T
Xg = Xg / np.linalg.norm(Xg, axis=0, keepdims=True)

Cd = abct.residualn(C, "degree")
Cg = Xg.T @ Xg

```

### Visualize residual structural and correlation networks

```{python}

neye = ~np.eye(len(data["W"]), dtype=bool) 

# Scatter plot of degree-corrected and rank-one subtracted structural networks
r = np.corrcoef(Wr[neye], Wd[neye])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(x=Wr[neye], y=Wd[neye])
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Network weights after first-component removal",
    yaxis_title="Network weights after degree correction",
    title=f"Structural networks (r = {r:.3f})",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

# Scatter plot of degree-corrected and global-signal regressed correlation networks
r = np.corrcoef(Cg[neye], Cd[neye])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(x=Cg[neye], y=Cd[neye])
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Network weights after global-signal regression",
    yaxis_title="Network weights after degree correction",
    title=f"Correlation networks (r = {r:.3f})",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

```
